svg.text: Spinneret Protocol — Complete Sequence Diagrams (All Commands + All NAT Cases)

g.text: 1. NAT Detection (0x00 + 0x01) → Login (0x02) — Detect Fixed/Variable NAT, then register
── Phase A: probe both ports to detect NAT type (0x00/0x01) ─────────────
── Phase B: send login with full PeerInfo (0x02) ──────────────────
Login updates global addr from packet source
Send every < 60s; GC every 60s removes expired peers
3. Filter Management — 0x04 SetFilter, 0x05 FilterInfo, 0x06 AddPeerFilter, 0x08 RemovePeerFilter
Whitelist: only listed peers can Spin us. Blacklist: listed peers cannot.
Filters are persistent (no TTL). Off by default.
4. Network Management — 0x09 RegisterNetwork, 0x0A NetworkInfo, 0x0C TangleNetwork, 0x0D JoinNetwork, 0x0B GetNetPeer
0xF6 AskHostJoin { net_id, joiner PeerInfo } → host B
Public: auto‑join. Private: host must approve (5s timeout).
5. Spin → Silk — Peer discovery + NAT hole punching + direct connection (0x0E)
Loom checks B's filter + reg status
── Phase 2: B calls accept(caller) → sends Echo to A directly ──
0xEB Entangle (Echo — B sends via direct UDP to A)
Both A and B now call make_silk() with each other's PeerInfo
── Phase 3: Simultaneous hole punch (strategy depends on NAT types) ───
0xEB Entangle every 50ms (both sides simultaneously to each other's addr)
0xEC EntangleAck — first reply received → connection established
0xEB every 50ms for 3s to ensure remote receives at least one packet
── Phase 4: Application data (direct peer‑to‑peer, no loom) ──────────
0x03 Tangle every < 60s to stay registered (for future Spins)
Silk idle ≥ 30s → send 0xEB; remote replies 0xEC (idle keepalive)
6. NAT Traversal Strategies — All four NAT‑type combinations + same‑router optimisation
Same‑Router Optimisation (both Fixed NAT, same global IP)
Try LAN address first → 0xEB / 0xEC (fall back to global if LAN fails)
Both send 0xEB to each other's IP:port every 50ms until reply received
Fixed sprays 0xEB to 101 ports (target ±50). Variable opens 100 sockets, sprays from all.
Variable also sends echo to Fixed's exact port. Timeout: 30s
Variable opens 100 sockets, sprays 0xEB from all to Fixed's single IP:port
Both open 100 extra sockets, each sprays 0xEB to 101 ports (±50) → 10,100 combos/side
Plaintext (after decryption): timestamp[8] | validity_secs[1] (1..10) | CMD[1] | payload[..]
Key agreement: X25519(receiver_priv, sender_pub) → shared_secret → ChaCha20‑Poly1305(shared_secret, nonce, plaintext)
Replay protection: (now ‑ timestamp) > validity_secs → packet rejected. Max validity: 10 seconds.
